_comment: Park factors for St. Louis Cardinals - VOS Evaluation
_comment_2: This file contains park adjustments for evaluating players specifically for the STL organization
_comment_3: Each team should have its own park factors file (e.g., park-factors-{team-code}.json)

raw_park_factors._comment: Raw park factors from OOTP - 1.0 = league average

park_profile.description: Slightly pitcher-friendly environment with notable home run suppression, especially for right-handed hitters.

tool_adjustments._comment: Multipliers applied to tool ratings. 1.0 = no change, >1.0 = more valuable, <1.0 = less valuable
tool_adjustments._comment_2: These are calculated from raw park factors using conversion formulas

defense._comment: Slightly lower XBH environment means mostly neutral defensive effects
defense._rationale_OFR: Lower triples rate slightly reduces outfield range pressure
defense._rationale_IFR: Only minor changes to balls in play profile overall

baserunning._comment: Slightly fewer extra-base outcomes marginally reduce speed value

pitcher_ability._comment: Home run suppression reduces differential value of HR-specific skills
pitcher_ability._rationale_Movement: Park already suppresses homers, reducing movement premium slightly
pitcher_ability._rationale_Control: Run prevention still rewards limiting baserunners
pitcher_ability._rationale_HR_Avoid: Park already suppresses HRs, so HR prevention specialists gain less relative value

handedness_splits._comment: Optional handedness-specific adjustments - only used if use_handedness_splits is true
handedness_splits._comment_2: Busch has meaningful split (RHB 0.810 vs LHB 0.960), so handedness splits are recommended
handedness_splits._rationale: Large split (~15%) makes handedness-aware power adjustments useful

application_rules._comment: Rules for when and how aggressively to apply these park factors
application_rules._comment_prospects: Apply to all players in St. Louis Cardinals organization, including minors
application_rules._comment_major_leaguers: Apply to players at ML level with this team
application_rules._comment_handedness: Enabled due to meaningful RHB/LHB HR suppression difference
application_rules._comment_strength: Use 50% strength as conservative starting point. 1.0 = full strength, 0.5 = half strength
application_rules._comment_strength_2: At 0.5 strength: Pow becomes 0.959 (4.1% reduction) instead of 0.918 (8.2% reduction)

validation_examples._comment: Test cases to verify park factors are working correctly

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.description: Compare 80 Pow / 45 Gap player vs 45 Pow / 80 Gap player

player_b_gap_hitter.impact: Gap profile is less penalized than pure power profile

power_hitter_vs_gap_hitter.result: Power-heavy profile loses more value than balanced/gap profile

usage_notes.creating_files_for_other_teams: Copy this file and adjust raw_park_factors and team_info sections
usage_notes.tuning_adjustment_strength: Start at 0.5, increase to 0.7-1.0 if you want more aggressive park effects
usage_notes.when_to_enable_handedness_splits: Enable when RHB/LHB HR factors differ by >10% (this park differs by ~15%)
